---
title: "Beer Game — Post-Game Analysis"
subtitle: "Classic Variant · Session `r params$game_id`"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    theme: cosmo
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
params:
  game_id: "unknown"
  data_file: "game_data.json"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
import json, math
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mtick
from pathlib import Path

with open(params["data_file"]) as f:
    data = json.load(f)

game_id   = data["game_id"]
n_rounds  = data["rounds_played"]
roles     = data["roles"]          # list of role names in supply-chain order
history   = data["round_history"]  # list of per-round dicts

df_cost = pd.DataFrame(
    {role: [r["cumulative_cost"][role] for r in history] for role in roles},
    index=range(1, n_rounds + 1),
)
df_cost.index.name = "Round"

df_inv = pd.DataFrame(
    {role: [r["inventory"][role] for r in history] for role in roles},
    index=range(1, n_rounds + 1),
)

df_orders = pd.DataFrame(
    {role: [r["orders"][role] for r in history] for role in roles},
    index=range(1, n_rounds + 1),
)

demand_series = [r["customer_demand"] for r in history]
```

## Game Summary

```{python}
final_costs = df_cost.iloc[-1].to_dict()
total_cost  = sum(final_costs.values())
winner      = min(final_costs, key=final_costs.get)

summary = pd.DataFrame({
    "Role": list(final_costs.keys()),
    "Final Cost ($)": [f"{v:.2f}" for v in final_costs.values()],
}).set_index("Role")

display(summary)
print(f"\nTotal supply-chain cost: ${total_cost:.2f}")
print(f"Lowest-cost role: {winner} (${final_costs[winner]:.2f})")
print(f"Rounds played: {n_rounds}")
```

## Cumulative Cost per Player

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4))
for role in roles:
    ax.plot(df_cost.index, df_cost[role], marker=".", label=role.capitalize())
ax.set_xlabel("Round")
ax.set_ylabel("Cumulative Cost ($)")
ax.set_title("Cumulative Cost per Role")
ax.legend()
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mtick.FormatStrFormatter("$%.0f"))
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Inventory Levels

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4))
for role in roles:
    ax.plot(df_inv.index, df_inv[role], marker=".", label=role.capitalize())
ax.axhline(0, color="red", linestyle="--", linewidth=0.8, label="Backlog boundary")
ax.set_xlabel("Round")
ax.set_ylabel("Inventory (units)")
ax.set_title("Inventory Over Time")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Bullwhip Effect

The bullwhip effect measures how demand variance amplifies up the supply chain.
A ratio > 1 for any upstream role indicates bullwhip behaviour.

```{python}
demand_var = max(pd.Series(demand_series).var(), 1e-9)

bw_ratios = {}
for role in roles:
    order_var = df_orders[role].var()
    bw_ratios[role] = order_var / demand_var

bw_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "Role": list(bw_ratios.keys()),
    "Order Variance / Demand Variance": [f"{v:.2f}x" for v in bw_ratios.values()],
}).set_index("Role")

display(bw_df)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 3))
ax.bar([r.capitalize() for r in roles], bw_ratios.values(), color="#2563eb")
ax.axhline(1.0, color="red", linestyle="--", linewidth=0.8, label="Demand baseline (1×)")
ax.set_ylabel("Variance Ratio")
ax.set_title("Bullwhip Effect: Order Variance / Customer Demand Variance")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Order vs Demand Patterns

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(len(roles), 1, figsize=(9, 2.5 * len(roles)), sharex=True)
rounds_idx = list(range(1, n_rounds + 1))
for ax, role in zip(axes, roles):
    ax.step(rounds_idx, df_orders[role], where="mid", label=f"{role.capitalize()} orders")
    if role == roles[0]:  # retailer sees actual demand
        ax.step(rounds_idx, demand_series, where="mid",
                linestyle="--", color="red", label="Customer demand")
    ax.set_ylabel("Units")
    ax.set_title(role.capitalize())
    ax.legend(fontsize=8)
axes[-1].set_xlabel("Round")
plt.suptitle("Orders Placed per Round (step chart)", y=1.02)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
